verses.text: Negev as a Direction ( Southward ) When the Torah describes motion toward the south direction, it uses the directional suﬃx -ָ ה –( ah ). Genesis 12:9 “And Abram journeyed, going on still toward the south.” Hebrew: “ וַיִּסַּע אַבְרָם הָלוֹךְ וְנָסוֹעַ הַנֶּגְבָּה ” Translation: “And Abram journeyed, going southward.” Here, ha-negbah ( הַנֶּגְבָּה ) = toward the south direction.” Genesis 13:1 “And Abram went up out of Egypt… into the south.” Hebrew: “ וַיַּעַל־אַבְרָם מִמִּצְרַיִם … הַנֶּגְבָּה ” Translation: “And Abram went up… southward.” Again, ha-negbah = directional, not territorial .

The Diﬀerence Made Plain ba-Negev ( בַנֶּגֶב ) = in the Negev land/region.” ha-negbah ( הַנֶּגְבָּה ) = toward the southward direction.” Thus the Torah itself preserves the truth: When Abram traveled after leaving Egypt (Genesis 13:1 ), he went southward — a direction pointing further into Africa . The later scribes, however, confused this with the desert region in PALESTINE and taught men falsely that the inheritance was northward .

The Seal of Factum Probanda The Hebrew tongue testiﬁeth. The witness of history agreeth. The times of Exodus and the Bantu migration align. Therefore, the inheritance of Israel is in the South, where milk and honey ﬂow. And the roar of the Lion ariseth from the South, never to be silenced again. NOW IN SIMPLE ENGLISH - MAYBE YOU MIGHT ACTUALLY OVERSTAND . The True Exodus Was to the South of Egypt Referencing Genesis (KJV) and the Hebrew Torah
And the Hebrew text sayeth: “ וַיַּעַל־אַבְרָם מִמִּצְרַיִם הוּא וְאִשְׁתּוֹ וְכָל־אֲשֶׁר־לוֹ וְלוֹט עִמּוֹ הַנֶּגְבָּה ”
The last word, ha-negbah ( הַנֶּגְבָּה ,) is not a place, but a direction — “ toward the south .”
When the Torah names a place, it says: “ ba-Negev ” ( בַּנֶּגֶב ) , meaning “in the Negev (region).”
But when it says “ ha-negbah ” with the ﬁnal - ah ending, it means motion toward the southward direction, not a settled land.
Thus Abram’s footsteps bear witness: his eyes were set to the great south, beyond the sands, into the abundance of the rivers and cattle. Chapter Two – The Error of the Scribes

And in later generations the scribes of the nations confused the direction with the place, saying that the Negev desert of Canaan was the inheritance. . Yet the Torah never spake so, for it distinguished with precision between Negev the land and Negbah the direction. . And the prophets bore witness that the blessing lay not in the waste places, but in the land where gold is found, and where the rivers run full in their season. . For the desert cannot ﬂow with milk and honey, but the Southland of Africa overﬂoweth with both.

Therefore the fathers walked southward, and the children shall return southward again. Chapter Three – The Exodus and the Migration . And in the days of Moses , the people departed out of Egypt, a great multitude, journeying by tribes and by families. . And the years of their wandering, as remembered by scribes and scholars, were counted by many; and the time of their going forth falleth between the ﬁfteenth and thirteenth century before the Christ . . In those days also began the great migration of the Bantu , southward out of the lands of the north, journeying with cattle, with iron, and with songs of their fathers. . Behold the times coincide, and the witnesses agree: the path of Exodus and the path of the Bantu were one river ﬂowing into the South . . And the stones of Great Zimbabwe and the voices of the Shona declare the same, that the covenant people found rest in the land of cattle and of honey, where the sun standeth in glory. Chapter Four – The Judgment of Truth . Therefore the true Exodus was to the South of Egypt, not to the Levant, nor to the dust of Canaan. . For the Torah itself testiﬁeth with its tongue, and the times of the nations conﬁrm with their records. . And the Spirit of remembrance bringeth forth the voice of the ancestors, saying: “This is the land I promised your fathers , a land of rivers and cattle, of lions and honeycomb.” . And the inheritance is conﬁrmed unto this day, sealed in the sun , and remembered in the bones of the people.
Thus the scroll is sealed : The true Exodus was southward , into the land of abundance.
And Negev is no mere desert of PALESTINE , but the signpost of direction, guiding the children into the Southern inheritance .

And the times of Exodus and the times of the Bantu migration are joined, for the covenant people are the same. . This is the word of remembrance, to be kept in the heart and declared in the gates. . For the South is the dwelling of the Throne , and the sun of righteousness ariseth from the South with healing in His wings. The True Exodus Was to the South of Egypt Referencing Genesis (KJV) and the Hebrew Torah The Law of the Tongue

Negev as a Location (Land) When the Torah speaketh of the Negev as a region, it useth the form ba-Negev ( בַנֶּגֶב .) Genesis 20:1 “And Abraham journeyed from thence toward the south country.” Hebrew: “ וַיִּסַּע מִשָּׁם אַבְרָהָם אַרְצָה הַנֶּגֶב ” Here, ha-Negev ( הַנֶּגֶב ) is a deﬁned territory. Numbers 13:17 “Go up this way southward, and go up into the mountain.” Hebrew: “ וַיֹּאמֶר עֲלֵה זֶה בַנֶּגֶב ” The form is ba-Negev, meaning “in the Negev land.”

Negev as a Direction (Southward) When the Torah declareth motion toward the south, it useth the directional suﬃx -ָ ה ah). Genesis 12:9 “And Abram journeyed, going on still toward the south .” Hebrew: “ וַיִּסַּע אַבְרָם הָלוֹךְ וְנָסוֹעַ הַנֶּגְבָּה ” ha-negbah ( הַנֶּגְבָּה ) = toward the southward direction.” Genesis 13:1 “And Abram went up out of Egypt … into the south .” Hebrew : “ וַיַּעַל־אַבְרָם מִמִּצְרַיִם … הַנֶּגְבָּה ” ha-negbah again = movement, not territory.

The Diﬀerence Made Plain ba-Negev ( בַנֶּגֶב ) = in the Negev (as land).” ha-negbah ( הַנֶּגְבָּה ) = toward the southward direction.” Thus the Torah itself divideth between place and path. Abram went southward, into Africa, not into the northern desert.

The Seal of Factum Probanda The Hebrew tongue beareth witness. The scroll of history agreeth. The days of Exodus and the days of the Bantu migration coincide. Therefore the true inheritance is in the South, where milk and honey ﬂow. And the roar of the Lion is from the South, to cover the whole earth. DECLARATION FROM THE THRONE OF THE SOUTH I am ABASID 1841 , the Exodus revealed in number and name. I am the Last Testament of ANI , the papyrus that breathes again. I am the True Lion of Judah , crowned not in the North, but in the South where the sun rises strong. I am the Sphinx , the eternal Watcher whose riddles silence the wise of nations. My number is written in Strong’s Concordance , and my path is carved in the Hebrew tongue: 1841 – exodos ( ἔ ξοδος): a going out, a departure, the Exodus. Behold, the scroll of remembrance testiﬁes that the true Exodus was to the South of Egypt, unto the land of rivers, cattle, gold, and honey. The times of the Exodus and the times of the Bantu migration are one; the witnesses agree. This is Factum Probanda — unshakable, eternal, sealed. THE SEVEN SPHINX RIDDLES RETURN Who was rejected, yet became the keeper of bread? Whose name is + + + , yet one covenant? Who walketh as Sun, as Serpent, and as Lion? What stone speaketh though it hath no tongue? What river drieth in the North but overﬂoweth in the South? What beast was slain, yet still roareth? Who is crowned with sun and moon, holding the covenant of times? Answer : ABASID 1841 – The Exodus, The Lion, The Sphinx. THE LAST TESTAMENT OF ANI ABASID 1841 ☀❤
